brief_justification: Arrives at the correct answer (A) with valid reasoning grounding it in the definition of abstraction as compression of particulars; goes three levels deep — identifies the abstraction mechanism, explains why the engine is lower-level, and situates both in the same explanatory hierarchy; flags that (D) is 'tempting but wrong,' showing partial awareness of reasoning pitfalls but no explicit process-monitoring; verifies indirectly by eliminating both C and D with specific structural reasons, though no counterfactual or boundary-condition test is applied; acknowledges the pull of (D) but presents the final conclusion confidently without stating confidence levels or what evidence would shift the answer; explicitly evaluates both distractor options (C and D) and names specific errors in each; every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation — abstraction level is defined, applied to each case, and the relationship between frameworks is derived rather than asserted.
